popularStory.description: Anchetil de Greye (c.1052 - post-1086) was a Norman follower of William FitzOsbern, 1st Earl of Hereford one of the great magnates of early Norman England and one of the very few proven companions of …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Anschetil de Grey於1130年出生時，他的父親Richard de Grey35歲，母親Mabilia de Redvers25歲。他於1150年與Eva Redvers在英國英格蘭奧可福爾康薇爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1162年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾洛瑟費爾德 格斯享年33歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

en.lifeSummary: When Anschetil de Grey was born in 1130, his father, Richard de Grey, was 35 and his mother, Mabilia de Redvers, was 25. He married Eva Redvers in 1150, in Cornwell, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1162, in Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anschetil de Grey est né en 1130, son père, Richard de Grey, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Mabilia de Redvers, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Eva Redvers en 1150, à Cornwell, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1162, à Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 33 ans.

Lui sposato Eva Redvers nel 1150, nel Cornwell, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Anschetil de Greyが1130年に生まれた時，父Richard de Greyは35歳，母Mabilia de Redversは25歳であった。1150年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアコルンウエルでEva Redversと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1162年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアロスエルフィールド グレイズで33歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Anschetil de Grey(이)가 1130년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Grey(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabilia de Redvers(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1150년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 코른웰에서 Eva Redvers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1162년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 로더필드 그리스에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anschetil de Grey родился в 1130 г., его отцу по имени Richard de Grey было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Mabilia de Redvers, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Eva Redvers в 1150 г. в местности корнвелл, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1162 г. в местности ротирфиелд грис, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 33 лет.
